reports.incident_description: New Year crowd formed a mixed queue at the main entrance; patients and visitors lined up together and blocked wheelchair access. Request reception to open a second OPD registration counter and announce token numbers over PA.
Brief morning rain left the foyer mats soaked; two visitors slipped near the help desk. Need reception to place wet-floor signs, direct people to the side entrance, and call housekeeping immediately.
Main switchboard failed to transfer external calls to ETU extensions after morning maintenance; emergency calls looped back to reception. Require immediate manual rerouting and directory fix.
Surgical review patients were directed to the medical clinic queue; the signage at the lobby arrowed to the wrong wing. Please correct wayfinding and announce the change on PA.
Initial insurance verification at reception missed policy numbers for two scheduled day-cases; HIS shows blank insurer fields. Kindly call the insurers and update profiles before admission.
Ambulance radioed ahead for a collapse case but ETU triage was not alerted by reception, causing a delay on arrival. Please reinforce the red-flag pre-alert protocol with operators.
Short afternoon rain brought in wet footprints; the OPD token queue extended across the damp foyer. Reception needs to redirect the queue and announce use of the drying mats.
Two outpatients arrived at X-ray with eChannelling references but had not completed registration at reception. Please register and issue OPD cards before sending patients to Radiology.
Evening visitor crowd exceeded the two-person rule; several passes were not issued at the desk. Request reception to enforce the pass system and guide overflow to the waiting area.
Registration slip carried the wrong age due to NIC DOB entry error; patient was sent to the incorrect fee category. Please correct in HIS and reprint the OPD chit.
Lobby queue display started flickering in the humid afternoon and patients crowded the counter for updates. Need manual token announcements and desk marshals until the screen stabilizes.
OPD cards arrived without new clinic numbers and reception stamps, delaying dispensing. Kindly re-stamp and ensure numbers are written at registration.
Patients reached Physio without prior registration or payment chits after being waved through by entrance volunteers. Request reception to brief volunteers and direct patients via proper intake.
Overnight calls were misrouted because the ward names changed (Surgical-2 now Ward 6B) but the extension list wasn’t updated. Please refresh the directory and on-call roster at reception.
Light rain crowded the entrance canopy and a vendor set up an umbrella stall inside the foyer. Reception needs to check vendor permission and relocate them outside the patient flow.
Caller reported an RTA inbound but was kept on hold during line transfer; ETU received the alert three minutes late. Please prioritise emergency lines at switchboard.
Relative wandered over an hour searching for Ward 4 due to faded signage; the map handed out at reception was outdated. Request updated printouts and an escort system for first-time visitors.
Only one OPD counter opened at morning rush; tokens were not issued by time slot and the line blocked wheelchair passage. Please open an extra counter and marshal the queue.
Intercom from reception to Paediatrics is not connecting; external calls cannot be transferred to our duty room. Need manual call patching and a fault report logged by switchboard.
Corporate admission pending because reception has not secured the Letter of Guarantee; patient is waiting in the lounge. Kindly call HR and the insurer to confirm LOG and update HIS.
Misty morning caused condensation on the entrance glass and the vaccine clinic sign was hard to read; guardians crowded the help desk. Request temporary floor arrows and a PA announcement.
Two walk-ins with chest pain reached the OPD counter but were not fast-tracked to ETU; no triage alert was made by reception. Immediate retraining on red-flag routing is required.

Light evening rain revealed a minor leak above the main desk; registration files became damp and staff got crowded. Need the desk shifted temporarily and maintenance informed, with lobby announcements.
Reception rescheduled two dialysis slots without checking our session timings; patients arrived after the machine cycle. Please align appointment changes with unit capacity and call patients.
A wallet handed to reception was not entered in the visitor property ledger; the owner is waiting with NIC. Kindly complete the log and release through the standard verification process.
Families followed an old foyer sign and ended up at ICU; front desk signage is outdated. Please redirect visitors and update the floor map at reception.
External donor hotline calls are being routed to OT via switchboard. Reception to correct the extension mapping and announce the proper line.
Ambulance pre-alert from 1990 Suwa Seriya rang the main line twice but ETU was not patched through. Reception must activate hot-transfer to ETU immediately.
OPD registration captured a wrong NIC digit; two same-name patients now have swapped labels. Need demographic correction and barcode reprint from front desk.
Token dispenser at OPD entrance jammed. Reception to issue manual tokens and call numbers over PA until fixed.
Agrahara pre-authorization was not initiated at registration for an admission. Please contact insurer and attach approval to the BHT.
Water filter maintenance vendor arrived without a gate pass. Reception to issue visitor badges and log tools as per entry protocol.
Evening light rain left the foyer floor wet; two visitors slipped near reception. Please arrange mopping, wet-floor signs, and a temporary queue reroute.
Tamil-only guardians in the OPD queue can’t find the Paeds clinic. Request Tamil-speaking reception staff and update bilingual direction slips.
eChannelling counter login failing; tokens are backing up into the corridor. Switch to manual appointment list and inform consultants through the groups.
Wrong DOB entered at registration for a cardiac OPD patient; insurer portal rejecting. Please amend in HIS and rerun eligibility.
No wheelchairs staged at the main entrance. Front desk to coordinate orderlies to park two chairs during OPD peak hours.
Thundery rain caused a power dip; the self-registration kiosk rebooted and lost today’s queue. Reception to recreate tokens from the pre-booked list.
Due to rain, attendants crowded under the canopy blocking the ambulance bay. Reception to disperse and redirect via PA and security.
Token slip header still shows yesterday’s date after midnight rollover. Front desk to correct system time and reissue affected tokens.
University tour group arrived unlogged. Reception to register 22 visitors, issue badges, and brief on photography restrictions.
Night switchboard didn’t forward to my mobile; two ward consults were missed. Reception to verify and update the after-hours call-forwarding list.
Morning drizzle pushed the taxi queue onto the disability ramp. Front desk to coordinate security to clear the path and manage arrivals.
Admission slip issued with an incorrect BHT number. Need reception to cancel and reassign to prevent chart merging.
Child’s school bag handed in at the counter. Please log in lost-and-found and announce every 30 minutes.
TV crew at entrance without Ministry clearance. Reception to hold at desk, inform Admin, and deny filming passes.
AIA pre-auth form scanned without policy number; claim will bounce. Reception to call guardian, capture details, and update HIS.
Brief evening rain with gusts caused the automatic sliding door sensor to fault; crowd forming. Reception to open the manual side door and guide flow.
After generator test during showers, PBX rebooted and external lines are down. Reception to initiate the manual call tree and share ETU’s mobile on the main line.
Appointment SMS batch went out with the wrong date for tomorrow’s clinics. Front desk to resend corrected messages and post a foyer notice.
New OPD registration linked to another patient's BHT due to similar names; Reception to correct demographics and unlink before triage.
During thunderstorm, ambulance ETA call rang twice without pickup on main switchboard; Reception must alert ETU immediately and monitor backup line.
Rain crowding caused visitors to queue into driveway; please open side entrance and deploy reception staff to redirect clinics.
Channelling counter issued duplicate token 34A to two children; Reception to reconcile order and announce correction to parents.
Agrahara beneficiary admission lacked initial insurance verification; Reception to obtain card and send pre-auth before bed allocation.
Post-rain lobby mats soaked; slip risk increased. Reception to cordon area and guide visitors through pharmacy corridor until housekeeping replaces.
Switchboard repeatedly routed ICU enquiry to Paediatrics; Reception to update extension list and brief afternoon operator.
Brief downpour closed parking gate; families crowded main doors. Reception to prioritize wheelchairs and create separate paediatric queue.
Passport details not captured at registration for foreign spouse; demographics need update before lab billing proceeds.
OPD token display frozen during morning rush; Reception to switch to manual calls and maintain queue log.
Incoming polytrauma alert reached reception first; please patch directly to ETU and time-stamp the call per protocol.
Diabetic clinic signage missing; patients arriving at radiology. Reception to station volunteer at foyer to direct until signage replaced.
Morning clinic lists not printed at reception; need immediate printing and placement at channelling counters before 07:30.
Two paediatric follow-ups registered under parents’ BHT numbers; Reception to split visits and assign correct child records.
Heavy rain and generator hum muffled PA; Reception please use handheld mic near pharmacy and guide queues.
Unbadged visitors reached OT corridor during peak hours; Reception to reinstate visitor badge issuance and logbook check at foyer.
Thunderstorm interference; I couldn’t reach the clinic via switchboard. Reception to reconnect and broadcast any delay to waiting patients.
Stroke alert called to reception; ETU not paged for 4 minutes. Immediate refresher on escalation protocol required.
Registration terminal printed lab slips with yesterday’s date; Reception to correct system time and reissue slips to affected patients.
Sudden rain pushed crowd against glass door; Reception to deploy queue barriers and direct overflow to OPD entrance.
Elderly Tamil-only patient confused at inquiries; please arrange Tamil-speaking clerk to escort to medical clinic.
Vendor truck blocked patient drop-off during drizzle; Reception to coordinate with security and redirect deliveries to rear gate.
Switchboard directory missing new Cath Lab extension; calls misrouted. Reception to update list and brief operators.
Wristband from registration showed ‘Penicillin allergy’ incorrectly; Reception to correct record and reprint before vitals.
Heavy rain backed families into foyer; vaccine clinic mixed with surgical line. Reception to separate queues and call tokens clearly.
Two surgical clinic patients registered as one due to identical names; OPD card/BHT merged. Reception to split records and reassign tokens before consult starts.
Lightning this evening causing main switchboard to drop inbound calls; trunks show busy. Request Reception to move to backup mobile and manually route urgent calls to wards.
Medical supplies vendor waiting without pass; needs verification and direction to CSSD. Reception please authenticate and issue visitor badge.
Ambulance from Kelaniya 10 mins out with polytrauma; no pre-alert on system. Reception please activate trauma pre-alert and inform triage/porters.
OPD token display frozen; new numbers not showing and crowd building. Reception to reset queue and make manual PA until fixed.
During storm, relatives' calls are being transferred to ICU direct line. Please apply night routing at switchboard and set brief IVR.
Patient for ultrasound sent to CT area due to lobby sign confusion. Request Reception to update directional boards and position a guide.
Brief downpour has visitors crowding foyer and blocking emergency exit. Need Reception to open overflow waiting and announce in Sinhala/Tamil.
New batch of interns arrived; no temporary access cards or extension list update. Reception to issue badges and update directory.
Power flicker in the storm jammed the token printer. Request Reception switch to manual tokens and inform clinics via PA.
Public calls for Clinic 5 being misrouted; extension on ePBX incorrect. Reception please correct routing and test both Sinhala/Tamil IVR options.
Corporate patient on afternoon list lacks initial pre-auth; insurer unreachable at ward. Reception to complete first-line verification via hotline.
Touchscreen self-registration kiosk unresponsive in the heat; queue stretching outside. Reception to open an extra counter and issue drinking-water advisory.
Avurudu visiting hours altered; callers/visitors unsure. Reception to update recorded greeting and place notices at gates.
Heavy rain causing vehicle pile-up at OPD drop-off; elderly patients stranded. Reception please coordinate wheelchairs and direct to covered entry.
Child registered under wrong guardian NIC; insurance pre-check failed. Request Reception to amend demographics and reprint OPD card.
Three patients claim phone-booked slots not on today's list. Reception to trace call logs and confirm appointments before access is prepared.
Suspected stroke transfer ETA 15 mins; no Code Stroke trigger yet. Reception please alert triage and CT immediately.
Lightning has taken down one trunk; main number intermittently unreachable. Reception to publicise alternate hotline and route via backups.
Channeling patients mixing with OPD cash queue; tokens issued incorrectly. Reception to separate flows and brief agents.
Evening rain has lobby overcrowded before visiting. Need Reception to start timed entry batches and make bilingual PA reminders.
External courier arrived at wrong gate with samples. Please Reception guide to Specimen Reception and alert security for handover.
Vendor for nebulizers here without prior appointment. Reception to verify credentials and connect to Biomedical office.
Night PBX profile wrong; calls for ETU landing in Labour Room. Reception please correct and test night routing now.
Due to widespread storms, two clinics cancelled; many patients already in queue. Reception to call/text lists, reassign tokens, and update boards.
Heavy downpour pushed crowds into the lobby; OPD queue merged with lab sample drop line. Reception needs to direct separate queues and announce token numbers.
Intermittent thunder causing line noise; calls to Paediatric Ward are not transferring. Backup mobile list is outdated—reception to update and route manually.
Flooded drop-off bay led visitors to crowd the main reception doors. Please deploy reception volunteers to manage flow and prioritize wheelchairs.
OPD self-registration kiosks were not powered on; patients queued at Counter 1. Request reception to open Counter 2 early and call out tokens manually.
Lightning tripped the token display above Reception; crowd is confused. Start manual announcements until the screen is reset.
Agrahara insurance verification desk is unmanned; two elective admissions are waiting for pre-admission stamps. Reception to arrange a standby clerk.
1990 Suwaseriya called—polytrauma ETA 12 minutes. ETU extension on the directory is unreachable; reception to overhead-page ETU immediately.
Widespread rain increased direction queries; volunteer desk is empty. Please assign one reception counter solely for wayfinding.
Registration created a duplicate MRN for a child due to an omitted NIC digit. Reception to merge records and reprint the OPD card.
Gusty winds kept the main doors open and queues spilled into the foyer. Reception to redirect entry via Gate B and manage the line.
Brief storm outage knocked external trunk lines. Switchboard should divert via the on-call mobiles/WhatsApp list until lines stabilize.
OPD token printer is jamming in humidity; numbers are faint. Reception to switch to pre-numbered chits and announce sequence.
Waterlogging near main reception is blocking wheelchair movement. Please assign reception staff to escort via the back corridor to lifts.
Tamil-speaking elders at registration; interpreter not on shift. Reception to page a Tamil-speaking staff/volunteer per the list.
Ambulance phoned ahead with suspected stroke; operator answered but ETU was not alerted. Implement an immediate 'red call' patch-through from reception.
During prolonged rain, visitors crowded reception asking for timings; the notice shows an old schedule. Please update and announce the current hours.
Switchboard still forwards calls to last month’s Surgery SHO. Reception to update the on-call directory immediately.
Storms causing repeated call drops from outstations. Reception to offer WhatsApp callback option and log messages.
Front desk gave wrong directions; a post-op patient climbed stairs to Ward 7 instead of using the lift. Please brief reception staff and fix arrow signage.
Police relayed bus accident details; the operator kept the caller on hold. Reception must patch through immediately to ETU and Duty MO.
Rain-soaked visitors waiting inside; pass printer ink is smearing in humidity. Reception to issue handwritten passes and log NICs.
Cloudburst led three-wheelers to crowd the porch; ambulance bay partly blocked. Reception to make a PA announcement and coordinate with security to clear the lane.
After 5:00 PM, cash counter phones are not routed from reception; patients are waiting for bills. Extend switchboard routing to 6:30 PM during the monsoon rush.
Family requested NIC correction; previous clerk entered the last digit wrong. Reception to update demographics and reprint the OPD card.
External hotline IVR still advertises the Saturday vaccine clinic that ended. Reception to update the recorded message today.
Morning downpour pushed crowds into foyer; floor wet near OPD registration. Request Reception to activate overflow queue at Counter 3, make PA announcement, and call housekeeping for caution boards.
Ambulance en route with polytrauma; please have Reception alert triage and clear wheelchair route from front porch to ETU immediately.
Sudden rain burst triggered spike in calls about clinic times; need Reception info desk to update recorded message and place extra staff for walk-ins.
With strong winds, visitors missed ENT vs Eye clinic; request Reception to post an usher at OPD junction and redirect to correct rooms.
Power flicker after thunderstorm jammed token printer; please have Reception issue manual tokens and inform patients of 20-min delay.
Antenatal crowd mixing with Child Immunization line; Reception to separate queues and place Sinhala/Tamil signage at stairwell.
Evening visitors keep approaching OPD desk for directions after hours; request Reception to enforce visiting-hours tokens and coordinate with security.
Clinic roster changed today; several follow-ups queued at general OPD. Reception to correct locator board and make PA announcement.
Heavy rain packed lift lobby; one elderly patient dyspnoeic. Reception to alert triage, deploy wheelchair, and guide X-ray patients via back corridor.
Lightning causing line noise; urgent external calls dropping. Reception to route ETU calls via mobile list and maintain manual log.
Token display blank in breezy lobby; Reception to switch to voice announcements and verify token order with counters.
Patients arriving at lab without OPD registration; please ask Reception to stamp OPD cards and send to lab only after demographic entry.
Caller reports stroke on the way; Reception/switchboard to keep line open, alert triage, and have stretcher ready at entrance.
Drizzle then downpour; NIC copier by Reception jammed, insurance check stalled. Reception to guide to alternate copier and note NIC numbers manually.
Due to wind and brief heavy rain many late arrivals; request Reception to extend clinic registration cut-off by 30 minutes and notify via PA.
Visitors clustering at entrance; Reception to issue visitor tokens and enforce one-attendant rule to reduce crowding.
During thunder and heavy rain, mother with lethargic child entered via foyer; Reception to alert triage and escort to ETU urgently.
Cleaning detour up; patients lost en route to Physio. Reception to station volunteer at lift lobby for one hour to guide.
Early downpour delayed staff; OPD counters short-handed. Reception to open backup counter and prioritize elderly/disabled.
Patients reaching pharmacy without OPD numbers; Reception to verify registration and reissue missing OPD cards.
Humidity causing token tickets to stick, double numbers issued; Reception to switch to manual token sheet and inform counters.
Heavy rain with thunder led to clinic room shift; patients still at old room. Reception to update locator board and send runner to redirect.
Multiple vendors calling to deliver; Reception to coordinate access time slots and direct them to loading bay, not main foyer.
Short intense rain drove private drop-offs to ambulance bay; Reception to place volunteer at porch and redirect vehicles to public drop-off.
Post-downpour crowding at information desk for surgical tokens; Reception to open temporary counter and provide bilingual guidance.

OPD queue spilled into the car park because only one registration counter opened. Reception did not activate the token system or deploy the float clerk; please open another counter and guide patients to counters 1–3.
Parent calls for NICU were repeatedly routed to Surgical Clinic by switchboard. Extension list appears outdated; request immediate correction and operator refresher.
Heavy morning rain drove crowds into the foyer; floor mats are soaked and slippery. Reception needs to redirect overflow to the OPD verandah and announce queue order to prevent slips.
Patient ‘Perera A.’ was registered twice due to a spelling variance; two HNs created. Reception to merge records and correct demographics before clinic call-up.
Patients for physio were sent to old Block C. Reception desk script still shows the outdated location; please update directions and the lobby map.
With steady rain, wet umbrellas are blocking the entrance line. Request reception to announce use of the umbrella rack and adjust queue ropes to keep pathways clear.
Review-only clinic patients were queued at general registration. Reception should fast-track per the pre-printed clinic list; delay now 45 minutes.
During the afternoon thunderstorm, calls transferred to OT kept dropping at the main switchboard. Please switch to backup routing and alert critical callers before disconnects.
Tamil-speaking patient was registered with the wrong address and phone due to language barrier. Need a Tamil-speaking registrar rostered at reception during peak hours.
Reception is sending Agrahara queries to Cashier. Initial insurance verification should be done at the front desk; our counter is getting blocked.
1990 ambulance called ahead; operator took details but triage was not alerted. Ambulance arrived unannounced. Require strict reception-to-ETU escalation adherence.
Reception issued general visitor badges for Antenatal instead of pink ward passes. Security turned visitors back; please correct badge color coding and brief the desk.
Change of venue for Dr. Jayasekara’s clinic was not updated at the enquiry desk. Around 30 patients waited at Room 6; need prompt announcements and redirection.
Two active extensions are listed for MOPD; callers are being bounced. Request an updated internal directory and guidance for routing after 4 PM.
Pharmaceutical rep arrived without prior appointment; reception did not log the visit or direct him to Procurement. Please enforce vendor sign-in and guidance.
Reception relayed bus accident casualty info late during rain and thunder. ETU was not prepped; require immediate overhead page and direct line alert from switchboard.
Multiple relatives are calling wards for visiting times. Reception is not handling general enquiries consistently; please provide a standard script or IVR message.
OPD token printer at reception jammed; number sequence lost and patients bypassed. Request manual token issuance and a written log until fixed.
Parent seeking advice for a neonate was routed to a random ward by operator. After-hours clinical calls must go to ETU/paeds on-call via switchboard priority.
Overnight gusts displaced the external OPD arrow sign. Morning visitors queued at Imaging; reception needs a greeter to redirect until signage is reset.
Reception sent patients to pharmacy before registration; prescriptions arrived without HN stickers. Please ensure the desk confirms HN generation first.
After a brief power dip, the lobby queue display showed yesterday’s clinic order. Reception continued to rely on it; please switch to manual announcements when screens desync.
Antenatal mothers were directed to general OPD instead of the CTG room near Labour Ward. Request desk use the updated directions and floor map.
Rain drove crowds into the foyer; reception shutters were down for lunch and enquiries were unattended. Please implement a staggered lunch roster for continuous coverage.
With widespread rain and high call volume, the operator kept surgeons on hold for over five minutes. Switchboard must apply the priority line protocol immediately.
Main lobby OPD token display froze; patients clustering and missing turns. Reception to reset the queue, make PA announcements, and call manually until fixed.
Light rain pushed visitors into the foyer; reception desk was unmanned during shift change. Need extra receptionist to direct to clinics and manage overflow seating.
Ambulance pre-alert came during evening thunder but switchboard did not patch immediately. Reception to activate red alert protocol and ring ETU backup line.
Rheumatology clinic shifted to Room 3A but reception signage still shows 2C. Please update boards and guide arrivals to avoid missed tokens.
Duplicate OPD registrations for one patient created two barcodes; samples queued twice. Reception to merge in HHIMS and retain correct BHT.
Family was directed to surgical OPD instead of paediatric clinic; child missed token. Reception to correct directions and fast-track the case.
Heavy rain brought water into the lobby; mats saturated. Reception to pause entry at main door, announce side corridor use, and coordinate mop-up.
Patients reaching pharmacy without printed clinic cards due to front desk printer jam. Reception to reprint and stamp cards to clear dispensing queue.
Insurance pre-authorization not initiated at admission. Reception to call insurer, upload forms, and note approval number to prevent non-payment.
PBX trunk 1 saturated with morning calls; outside callers cannot reach wards. Reception to route via trunk 2 and circulate alternate contact list.
NIC entered incorrectly at registration; lab results not linking to the chart. Reception to correct demographics in HHIMS and reissue barcode labels.
During rain, an ambulance ETA was received on the general line but ETU alert was not triggered. Reception to follow 'Code Red Arrival' paging protocol.
New referrals told to queue at OPD counter instead of Physio registration in Basement. Reception to brief volunteers and redirect patients properly.
Gusty rain forced family groups into the lobby, blocking the trolley route. Reception to manage visitor flow and issue ICU visiting time tokens.
Unable to reach Paediatrics via listed extension; switchboard directory is outdated. Reception to update the extension map and route calls correctly.
HHIMS intermittent at front desk; manual registrations piling up. Reception to activate downtime protocol and issue handwritten OPD tokens.
Two inpatients sent for ultrasound without registration stickers/receipts. Reception to verify, stamp request forms, and redirect patients properly.
Elderly Tamil-only speaker lost near lobby during thundery rain. Reception to arrange interpreter and escort to cardiology clinic.
Relatives attempting to visit ICU outside hours causing corridor crowding. Reception to enforce visiting policy and manage pass issuance.
After thunder, queue display screens are flickering at the entrance. Reception to switch to manual calling and use PA announcements.
Channelling counter issued tokens for a clinic cancelled due to consultant’s weather delay. Reception to inform, rebook, and validate refunds.
Paying OPD patient registered under free category; billing rejected. Reception to correct category in HHIMS and reprint invoice.
Pre-op patient arrived in theatre without BHT created. Reception to generate BHT and attach labels before transfer proceeds.
Heavy rain led ride-hailing cars to queue at the drop-off, blocking the ambulance bay. Reception to make PA announcements and coordinate clearing.
External referral doctor kept on hold; switchboard failed to patch to ETU. Reception to use emergency override and return the call immediately.
OPD token display at Reception froze; queue spilled into the foyer. Front desk issued handwritten tokens and had to guide patients to counters.
NIC scanner at Registration intermittently failed in the humidity; demographic entry slowed and patients were bounced between desks. Reception to switch to manual entry and check the device.
Evening rain pushed visitors into the atrium; soaked mats and a queue across the doorway created slip risk. Reception needed to redirect foot traffic and place wet-floor cones.
Consultant clinic pre-booked list did not sync with Reception; booked patients were sent back to OPD. Front desk reconciliation is needed before clinics open.
Ambulance pre-alert rang the main line with no pickup for ~3 minutes; a suspected stroke arrived unannounced. Require backup operator cover during breakfast break.
Imaging payment receipts at Reception would not print; thermal paper was damp from the afternoon rain. X-ray patients waited until rolls were changed.
Storm-related power flicker rebooted the PABX; inbound calls dropped for 8 minutes. Need surge protection and rapid reroute to backup mobiles.
Switchboard kept routing overseas callers to cardiac theatre instead of the cardiology ward. Please update extension labels and provide a quick reference for operators.
Reception entered an incorrect policy number during initial insurance verification; an elective surgery was nearly billed as self-pay. Need a double-check step before admission stickers are printed.
GP called about an incoming anaphylaxis but was put on hold by the operator during the thunderstorm; ED was not patched immediately. Require direct critical-call transfer protocol.
Two OPD patients with the same name were registered under one MRN at Reception; lab results attached to the wrong encounter. Immediate record split and OPD notification needed.
OPD announcements were only in English; Sinhala/Tamil speakers at the front desk missed tokens and counters. Reception should enable trilingual announcements and clearer displays.
Vendor delivering oxygen regulators bypassed Reception and entered OT stores without badges; visit not logged at the desk. Need stricter vendor sign-in and escort from Reception.
Heavy rain and generator hum made foyer PA announcements inaudible; patients queued at Cashier instead of Registration. Reception to raise PA volume and station a temporary usher.
Flooded car park left NIC cards wet; the registration scanner jammed and intake stalled. Reception to switch to manual intake and provide plastic sleeves at entry.
After-hours operator used an outdated rota for surgical on-call; consent was delayed. Switchboard must use the current contact list and confirm read-backs.
Clinic relocated to Block C, but Reception kept directing patients to the old OPD rooms. Update wayfinding at the main desk and on the token display.
During a thundery shower and visiting hour, the foyer was packed; Reception issued OPD tokens to visitors. Need separate queues and clear signage for visitors vs patients.
Gate lodge intercom to Reception failed in the evening storm; ambulance arrival was not relayed to triage promptly. Require a dedicated emergency mobile at the desk.
eChannelling list printed without MRNs; front desk re-registered follow-ups, creating duplicates. Immediate merges and a known-patient check-in workflow are required.
Family looking for an ICU patient was directed to HDU by Reception; they became distressed. Need location cards and a verification question before directing.
Front desk advised that the OPD cashier was closed and redirected payments to pharmacy; queues overflowed and delayed dispensing. Reception to align closing times and announcements.
Morning rain but no entrance mat or queue barrier at Reception; first-step area was slippery though no fall occurred. Front desk to deploy mats and cones proactively.
Switchboard was overloaded; a mother seeking directions could not reach Maternity despite three attempts. Need overflow routing or recorded directions in Sinhala/Tamil.
Corporate screening group arrived together; Reception lacked a group registration process and tokenised individually, backing up OPD by 45 minutes. Please institute bulk pre-registration at the desk.
During the evening thunderstorm the main PABX kept dropping external calls; I had to redial ETU several times to pass messages. Need reception backup routing list activated.
Ambulance pre-alert from 1990 was not relayed promptly from reception due to PA failure in the lobby. Request immediate manual runner protocol during storms.
Heavy rain caused crowding at the entrance and the OPD token kiosk was unplugged; patients confused about queue order. Reception to shift to manual tokens and guide seniors separately.
OPD registration keyed wrong NIC for a new patient creating a duplicate PHN. Please correct demographics and circulate a quick check SOP to reception counters.
Main number is intermittently routing to Pharmacy instead of reception after hours. Switchboard to review PABX night service settings.
Severe lightning—pre-alert call for a polytrauma dropped and ETU was not informed until arrival. Reception must use ETU mobile and backup list during outages.
Rainwater tracked into the foyer; no wet floor cones near reception queue and two visitors nearly slipped. Please coordinate housekeeping and redirect foot traffic.
Switchboard overloaded during the thunderstorm; calls to Ward 12P not getting through. Need an extra reception staffer to triage urgent ward extensions.
Relatives of a trauma patient were sent to the wrong lift bank because signage wasn’t updated at reception during lift maintenance. Please brief desk staff and update wayfinding.
Persistent rain led to long registration lines spilling outdoors; elderly and antenatal patients standing in rain. Reception to open Counter 3 and fast-track vulnerable groups.
Due to flooded roads several consultants are late; hotline keeps ringing out. Reception to post delay notice at main desk and reschedule slots.
Patients for the Eye OPD are still being directed to the old block. Reception needs to consistently direct them to Annex B, Level 2.
Power dip during the storm knocked out reception printers; manual OPD forms ran out. Need immediate supply and guidance on temporary numbering.
Visiting hours changed today but lobby crowd was not informed. Please make PA announcements from reception and manage the flow to wards.
Wind blew down the temporary ETU Entrance board; ambulances diverted to the main porch. Reception to station a staffer to redirect until signage is fixed.
Oxygen cylinder vendor arrived during the evening rain but was not logged at reception, delaying handover to Stores. Please clear the vendor queue and stamp passes.
Caller with chest pain was transferred by switchboard to Cardiology OPD instead of ETU. Reception must follow the emergency call script immediately.
Lightning took down one trunk line; external calls are failing. Need reception to divert main number to DMO mobile and post status at the front desk.
VIP visitor arrived without reception notifying administration as per protocol. Please reinforce the visitor alert SOP at the main desk.
Brief flooding at the front ramp; wheelchair users were sent via a route with steps. Reception to guide through the service corridor and inform lift operators.
Insurance verification for a day-case hernia repair took 45 minutes at information desk; theatre list started late. Reception to prioritize pre-authorized cases.
Waiting area AC down; patients restless. Reception should slow OPD token release and arrange drinking water near the main desk.
Intercom to wards is noisy during the storm; reception cannot hear extensions clearly. Use the backup call tree and confirm handovers by callback.
Torrential rain flooded the foyer; reception rerouted foot traffic through the ambulance bay, obstructing ETU access. Need marshals and tape to create a safe alternate path.
Month-end batch closed the main card machine at reception; patients queued for cash. Please switch to the backup terminal and display a notice.
Wet lobby mats from morning drizzle made the foyer slippery; please have Reception redirect foot traffic via the side OPD entrance and place clear caution signage.
During heavy rain a caller reported a breathless patient in a car at the gate; the main board did not alert ETU immediately. Reception must escalate such calls to triage without delay.
After a brief power flicker the token display at OPD Reception went blank; request Reception to shift to manual tokens and announce counter numbers to avoid registration delays.
A vendor entered without a visitor pass and was moving near the counters; Reception should issue a vendor badge and direct them to the correct collection point.
Thunderstorms caused line noise and external calls kept bouncing to Surgical Ward; need Reception to manually route critical calls and keep a call log until lines stabilize.
Announcements for tokens were only in Sinhala; Tamil-speaking patients missed their turns. Reception to use bilingual PA for OPD counters.
Insurance verification desk was unmanned during lunch due to rain delays; please arrange Reception relief staff and display a temporary notice for queue management.
OPD and Lab queues merged into one crowd around the front desk at midday; Reception needs to split the lines and direct patients to the correct counters.
PA system in the foyer failed after the thunder; Reception should switch to the battery megaphone and send WhatsApp text alerts to clinics for token ranges.
During the evening rain, taxis crowded the covered drop-off and blocked patient access; Reception to make periodic announcements and coordinate the queue.
A patient’s district code and DOB were entered incorrectly at registration; specimen label didn’t match the MRN. Reception to correct demographics and reprint labels.
Morning rainwater pooled in the foyer making the regular queue space unusable; Reception should shift registration to the side counter and guide patients accordingly.
Peripheral hospital phoned ahead about a suspected stroke but the alert from the main board to ETU was delayed; Reception must use the red-flag protocol immediately.
An elderly visitor got lost between OPD and X-ray; Reception should provide an escort and update the wayfinding directions at the help desk.
A walk-in casualty family reported at the front desk but triage was not alerted for several minutes; Reception to activate the emergency call to ETU immediately.
Only one registration counter was open during tea break and the line overflowed into the corridor; Reception should stagger breaks and open an auxiliary counter.
Internal intercom to wards is down; Reception must patch urgent calls via listed mobiles and maintain a manual call-routing sheet.
Digital board at Reception showed Sunday visiting hours on a weekday; visitors argued at entrances. Please correct the display and brief visitors.
A busload of clinic patients arrived early due to schedule changes in heavy rain; Reception to start pre-registration and open extra counters to prevent crowding.
Leak from the foyer roof dripped onto the token kiosk causing a shutdown; Reception should switch to manual tokens and announce counter calls until repaired.
Main line received many wrong-number calls meant for a private lab listed online; Reception to update the IVR message and redirect queries appropriately.
Foyer AC was out in hot, muggy conditions; the queue became congested. Reception should shift patients to the shaded veranda and manage the line.
Child’s MRN not found due to registration under a misspelled name and wrong DOB; Reception to merge duplicates and correct demographics before clinic.
Family phoned the main board to pre-alert a polytrauma ambulance during the storm; call stayed in queue. Reception must fast-track ETU connection on such alerts.
Two foreign visitors asking about private admissions were given OPD tokens; Reception should direct such inquiries to the private wing desk and provide fee info.
Heavy rain soaked the entrance mats; two visitors nearly slipped. Reception to place wet-floor signs and call housekeeping to mop the lobby.
Thunderstorm causing intermittent trunk line drops; an ambulance pre-alert failed. Switchboard to route via backup mobiles and notify ETU immediately.
OPD token display froze while queue extended outside. Reception to reset the display and open the overflow seating area.
Radiology has shifted to Block B, but lobby signage still points to old corridor. Reception to post temporary arrows and brief front-desk staff.
NIC card scanner at registration is not reading—humidity causing repeated errors. Reception to switch to manual log and arrange vendor check.
Early bus group for diabetic clinic arrived together; counters not open yet. Reception to open an extra counter and alert triage for flow.
Evening rain pushed crowds into the lobby; queues merged near lifts. Reception to direct visitors to correct wards and coordinate security barriers.
External lab courier is waiting at front desk with samples; lab not called. Reception to ring Lab Ext. 210 and log handover.
Afternoon storm causing multiple dropped external calls to clinics. Reception to activate the backup contact list and route via staff mobiles.
Registration rejecting old‑format NICs with the letter suffix. Reception to guide staff on old vs 12‑digit formats and request IT to adjust validation.
Male at Gate 1 reporting chest pain en route to ETU. Reception to dispatch a wheelchair/porter and pre-alert ETU.
Ceiling drip near main desk after evening rain; forms box getting wet. Reception to shift the counter forward and place caution signs while maintenance arrives.
eChannelling list doesn’t match hospital registry; several duplicate bookings for ENT clinic. Reception to reconcile names and renumber tokens.
Visitor badge printer ribbon nearly finished; prints are faint. Reception to replace ribbon before 4–6 pm visiting.
Ambulance pre-alert through reception landline was distorted during rain. Reception to confirm via mobile and alert ETU without delay.
Supplier with oxygen cylinders waiting at entrance unsure of route. Reception to guide to Main Stores and inform Biomedical Unit.
Lobby information board shows old year-end clinic times. Reception to update timings and make a short PA announcement.
Two Tamil-speaking elders at desk; no Tamil-speaking receptionist on shift. Reception to arrange interpreter support and direct them to Medical Clinic counter.
Patient’s clinic book handed in last night; owner not traced. Reception to check the lost-and-found log and call OPD Records.
Volunteer choir set up across the token queue; patients are confused. Reception to reroute the line and brief the group.
Unusually high holiday call volume about visiting hours; lines congested. Reception to enable recorded message and add a relief operator.
Token printer jammed; paper curled due to humidity. Reception to clear the jam and switch to manual token slips.
Rain tracked in from ambulance bay making floor slick near helpdesk. Reception to cordon area and call housekeeping.
Phone directory lists wrong extensions for Wards 5B and 6A; callers misrouted. Reception to update the switchboard directory and circulate.
Voicemail inbox on main line is full from last night; callers can’t leave messages. Reception to clear messages and test routing.
